revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表| name = 内山城| pref = 長野県| ar_called = | struct = 山城| tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑?| build_y = [[永正]]年間?| revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]]| reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？| remains = なし| location = | }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本]]の[[城]]。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。
[[天文 (年号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に[[武田信玄|武田晴信（信玄）]]は[[佐久郡]]に攻め入った。当時の内山城は[[大井貞清]]が城主であったが、降伏して武田氏に臣従した。 晴信は内山城の城代に[[小山田昌辰|上原伊賀守昌辰（小山田備中守昌辰）]]を任じた。
天文17年（[[1548年]]）、[[上田原の戦い]]に勝利した[[村上義清]]が佐久郡に侵入した内山城を攻めたが、晴信が諏訪から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略された後、廃城になった。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=36/14/02.170&el=138/31/41.823&scl=70000&bid=Mlink&coco=36/14/02.170,138/31/41.823&icon=home,,,,, 内山城の位置を示した地図]
==関連項目== *[[日本の城一覧#長野県|日本の城一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うちやましよう}} [[Category:長野県の城]] [[Category:長野県の史跡]] [[Category:佐久市]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の城郭概要表| name = 内山城| pref = 長野県| ar_called = | struct = 山城| tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑?| build_y = [[永正]]年間?| revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]]| reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？| remains = なし| location = | }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本]]の[[城]]。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に[[武田信玄|武田晴信（信玄）]]は[[佐久郡]]に攻め入った。当時の内山城は[[大井貞清]]が城主であったが、降伏して武田氏に臣従した。 晴信は内山城の城代に[[小山田昌辰|上原伊賀守昌辰（小山田備中守昌辰）]]を任じた。
天文17年（[[1548年]]）、[[上田原の戦い]]に勝利した[[村上義清]]が佐久郡に侵入した内山城を攻めたが、晴信が諏訪から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略された後、廃城になった。

== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に[[武田信玄|武田晴信（信玄）]]は[[佐久郡]]に攻め入った。当時の内山城は[[大井貞清]]が城主であったが、降伏して武田氏に臣従した。 晴信は内山城の城代に[[小山田昌辰|上原伊賀守昌辰（小山田備中守昌辰）]]を任じた。
天文17年（[[1548年]]）、[[上田原の戦い]]に勝利した[[村上義清]]が佐久郡に侵入した内山城を攻めたが、晴信が諏訪から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。

{{日本の城郭概要表| name = 内山城| pref = 長野県| ar_called = | struct = 山城| tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑?| build_y = [[永正]]年間?| revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]]| reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？| remains = なし| location = | }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本]]の[[城]]。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。
甲斐国の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は天文10年から[[信濃侵攻]]を開始し、[[諏訪郡]]を征圧すると[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）には佐久郡へ侵攻し、同年5月3日には内山城攻めを行い、城主[[大井貞清]]は降伏して武田氏に臣従した（『[[高白斎]]記』）。内山城代には一時大井貞清に任されるが、同年7月18日に武田家臣の[[小山田虎満|上原伊賀守]]（小山田備中守、虎満）が城代となり（『高白斎記』）、虎満は死去まで城代を務めている。天文17年（[[1548年]]）2月14日には、[[小県郡]]上田原における[[上田原の戦い]]において武田方を撃退した[[村上義清]]は内山城を攻めたが、晴信は諏訪[[上原城]]から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。

[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。

{{日本の城郭概要表| name = 内山城| pref = 長野県| ar_called = | struct = 山城| tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑?| build_y = [[永正]]年間?| revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]]| reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？| remains = なし| location = | }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本の城]]。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。
甲斐国の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は天文10年から[[信濃侵攻]]を開始し、[[諏訪郡]]を征圧すると[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）には佐久郡へ侵攻し、同年5月3日には内山城攻めを行い、城主[[大井貞清]]は降伏して武田氏に臣従した（『[[高白斎記]]』）。内山城代には一時大井貞清に任されるが、同年7月18日に武田家臣の[[小山田虎満|上原伊賀守]]（小山田備中守、虎満）が城代となり（『高白斎記』）、虎満は死去まで佐久郡の[[郡代]]を務めている。天文17年（[[1548年]]）2月14日には、[[小県郡]]上田原における[[上田原の戦い]]において武田方を撃退した[[村上義清]]は内山城を攻めたが、晴信は諏訪[[上原城]]から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=36/14/02.170&el=138/31/41.823&scl=70000&bid=Mlink&coco=36/14/02.170,138/31/41.823&icon=home,,,,, 内山城の位置を示した地図]

{{日本の城郭概要表| name = 内山城| pref = 長野県| ar_called = | struct = 山城| tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑?| build_y = [[永正]]年間?| revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]]| reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？| remains = 曲輪| cultural asset=未指定| location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}}| 地図=Japan Nagano| ラベル=内山城| 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9| 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7| アイコン=日本の城跡| ラベル位置=bottom }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本の城]]。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。戦国時代の城主は信濃国衆・岩村田大井氏。
[[甲斐国]]の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は天文10年から[[信濃侵攻]]を開始し、『[[高白斎記]]』によれば[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年（[[1543年]]）には信濃[[長窪城]]主・[[大井貞隆]]は武田方に捕縛された。貞隆の子息・[[大井貞清|貞清]]が内山城において抵抗し、天文15（[[1546年]]）には武田氏は佐久郡へ侵攻し、同年5月3日には内山城攻めを行う。『高白斎記』によれば、同年5月21日に貞清は降伏し、野沢（[[佐久市]]）へ退いた。『高白斎記』によれば、貞清は翌天文16年に武田氏に臣従した。
内山城代には一時大井貞清に任されるが、同年7月18日に武田家臣の[[小山田虎満|上原伊賀守]]（小山田備中守、虎満）が城代となり（『高白斎記』）、虎満は死去まで佐久郡の[[郡代]]を務めている。天文17年（[[1548年]]）2月14日には、[[小県郡]]上田原における[[上田原の戦い]]において武田方を撃退した[[村上義清]]は内山城を攻めたが、晴信は諏訪[[上原城]]から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後、天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = 内山城 | pref = 長野県 | ar_called = | struct = 山城 | tower_struct = | builders = 大井美作入道玄岑? | build_y = [[永正]]年間? | revamp = | rulers = [[大井貞清]] | reject_y = [[天正]]11年（[[1583年]]）？ | remains = 曲輪 | cultural asset=未指定 | location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | 地図=Japan Nagano | ラベル=内山城 | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=日本の城跡 | ラベル位置=bottom }} '''内山城'''（うちやまじょう）は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]]内山にあった[[日本の城]]（[[山城]]）。
== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。戦国時代の城主は信濃国衆・[[大井氏#大井氏（小笠原氏族）|岩村田大井氏]]。
[[甲斐国]]の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は天文10年から[[武田信玄#信濃攻め|信濃侵攻]]を開始し、『[[高白斎記]]』によれば[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年（[[1543年]]）には信濃[[長窪城]]主・[[大井貞隆]]は武田方に捕縛された。貞隆の子息・[[大井貞清|貞清]]が内山城において抵抗し、天文15（[[1546年]]）には武田氏は佐久郡へ侵攻し、同年5月3日には内山城攻めを行う。『高白斎記』によれば、同年5月21日に貞清は降伏し、野沢（[[佐久市]]）へ退いた。『高白斎記』によれば、貞清は翌天文16年に武田氏に臣従した。
内山城代には一時大井貞清に任されるが、同年7月18日に武田家臣の[[小山田虎満|上原伊賀守]]（小山田備中守、虎満）が城代となり（『高白斎記』）、虎満は死去まで佐久郡の[[郡代]]を務めている。天文17年（[[1548年]]）2月14日には、[[小県郡]]上田原における[[上田原の戦い]]において武田方を撃退した[[村上義清]]は内山城を攻めたが、晴信は諏訪[[上原城]]から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後、天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。
== 参考文献 == *[[信濃史学会]]編 『信州の山城 信濃史学会研究叢書2』 1988年 *[[南原公平]] 著『信州の城と古戦場』 [[しなのき書房]] 2009年
== 外部リンク == *[https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 内山城の位置を示した地図]mapion

== 概要 == 内山城は、[[永正]]年間に大井美作入道玄岑が築城したといわれている。戦国時代の城主は信濃国衆・[[大井氏#大井氏（小笠原氏族）|岩村田大井氏]]。
[[甲斐国]]の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は天文10年から[[武田信玄#信濃攻め|信濃侵攻]]を開始し、『[[高白斎記]]』によれば[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年（[[1543年]]）には信濃[[長窪城]]主・[[大井貞隆]]は武田方に捕縛された。貞隆の子息・[[大井貞清|貞清]]が内山城において抵抗し、天文15（[[1546年]]）には武田氏は佐久郡へ侵攻し、同年5月3日には内山城攻めを行う。『高白斎記』によれば、同年5月21日に貞清は降伏し、野沢（[[佐久市]]）へ退いた。『高白斎記』によれば、貞清は翌天文16年に武田氏に臣従した。
内山城代には一時大井貞清に任されるが、同年7月18日に武田家臣の[[小山田虎満|上原伊賀守]]（小山田備中守、虎満）が城代となり（『高白斎記』）、虎満は死去まで佐久郡の[[郡代]]を務めている。天文17年（[[1548年]]）2月14日には、[[小県郡]]上田原における[[上田原の戦い]]において武田方を撃退した[[村上義清]]は内山城を攻めたが、晴信は諏訪[[上原城]]から佐久へ出馬しこれを退けた。
[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）に武田氏が滅亡した後、天正11年（[[1583年]]）に[[徳川家康]]の麾下[[依田信蕃]]らによって攻略され廃城になった。
== 参考文献 == *[[信濃史学会]]編 『信州の山城 信濃史学会研究叢書2』 1988年 *[[南原公平]] 著『信州の城と古戦場』 [[しなのき書房]] 2009年

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内山城|version=98986497|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].

Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.

However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.

<!--Uchiyama Castle--><!--Uchiyama Castle--> [[Category:Buildings in Saku]] [[Category:History of Saku]] [[Category:信濃国の城]] [[Category:信濃大井氏]] [[Category:現存しない長野県の建築物]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11393957]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11393957]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.

== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11393957]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11393957]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] (山城, mountain castle) located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Uchiyama]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Harunobu]] (later known as Takeda Shingen) from [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]]). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] of [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]] launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the [[:en:Battle of Uedahara|Battle of Uedahara]]. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu's]] retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:قلعه اوچی‌یاما]][[ja:内山城]]
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|WD=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle}} (山城, mountain castle) located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Uchiyama}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Harunobu}} (later known as Takeda Shingen) from {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|WD=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo}} of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|en|Battle of Uedahara|WD=Q1358091|lt=Battle of Uedahara}}. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu's}} retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|WD=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo|1=Murakami Yoshikiyo}} of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|en|Battle of Uedahara|WD=Q1358091|lt=Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Uedahara}}. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu's|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)

revision.text:
{{日本の城郭概要表 | name = Uchiyama Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | ar_called = | struct = Mountain Castle | tower_struct = | builders = Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen? | build_y = [[Eishō (era)|Eishō]] era? | revamp = | rulers = [[Ōi Sadakiyo]] | reject_y = [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 11 (1583)? | remains = kuruwa (enclosure) | cultural asset=Unspecified | location = {{coordinates|36|14|13.9|N|138|31|30.7|E|scale:20000}} | location map=Japan Nagano | ラベル=Uchiyama Castle | 緯度度=36|緯度分=14|緯度秒=13.9 | 経度度=138|経度分=31|経度秒=30.7 | アイコン=Ruins of a Japanese castle | ラベルlocation=bottom }} '''Uchiyama Castle''' (内山城, Uchiyama-jō) was a {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|WD=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle|1=Japanese castle}} (山城, mountain castle) located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Uchiyama|1=Saku, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Uchiyama Castle is said to have been constructed by Ōi Mimasaka Nyūdō Gensen during the [[Eishō (era)|Eishō era]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the castle lord was the Ōi clan of the [[Iwamurada Ōi clan]] from Shinano Province.
In the [[Tenbun (era)|Tenbun]] 10th year, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Harunobu|1=Takeda Shingen}} (later known as Takeda Shingen) from {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}} began his invasion of Shinano ([[Takeda Shingen#Shinano Campaign|Shinano Campaign]]). According to the ''[[Kōhakusaiki]]'', in the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), [[Ōi Sadataka]], the lord of [[Nagakubo Castle]], was captured by Takeda forces. His son, [[Ōi Sadakiyo]], resisted at Uchiyama Castle. By the 15th year of Tenbun (1546), Takeda forces invaded Saku District and attacked Uchiyama Castle on May 3. As recorded in the ''Kōhakusaiki,'' Sadakiyo surrendered on May 21 and retreated to Nozawa (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City|1=Saku, Nagano}}). The following year, in the 16th year of Tenbun, Sadakiyo submitted to the Takeda.
Although Sadakiyo was temporarily reinstated as the castle warden, on July 18 of the same year, Takeda vassal [[Oyamada Toramitsu|Uehara Iga no Kami]] (Oyamada Bitchū no Kami, Toramitsu) was appointed as the castle warden (as per ''Kōhakusaiki''). Toramitsu served as the district warden of Saku until his death. On February 14 of the 17th year of Tenbun (1548), {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|WD=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo|1=Murakami Yoshikiyo}} of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} launched an attack on Uchiyama Castle after defeating Takeda forces at the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|en|Battle of Uedahara|WD=Q1358091|lt=Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Uedahara}}. However, Harunobu marched from Suwa [[Uehara Castle (Suwa)|Uehara Castle]] to Saku and repelled the assault.
After the fall of the Takeda clan in [[Tenshō (era)|Tenshō]] 10 (1582), Uchiyama Castle was attacked by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu's|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} retainer [[Yoda Nobunari]] in the following year, Tenshō 11 (1583), and subsequently abandoned.
== References == * [[Shinano Historical Association]] (ed.), ''Shinano no Yamajiro: Shinano Historical Association Research Series 2'', 1988. * [[Nanbara Kohei]], ''Shinano no Shiro to Kosenshō'', [[Shinanoki Shobō]], 2009.
== External Links == * [https://www.mapion.co.jp/m2/36.23693522272908,138.52540967920342,13 Map of Uchiyama Castle's location] (Mapion)
